message:
feat(factory,codex,gemini): extract tool calls/results and improve metric accuracy

Factory provider now parses tool_use/tool_call/function_call content blocks
and message-level toolCalls/toolResults arrays, so session listings show
accurate tool-use counts instead of zeroes. This brings Factory on par with
the existing Codex and Claude Code extraction logic.

Codex provider gains payload-level function_call and function_call_output
parsing for rollout formats that embed tool invocations at the envelope level
rather than inside content arrays. Includes helper for parsing stringified
JSON arguments.

Gemini provider falls back to the `thoughts` field when `content` is empty,
which is common in tool-heavy Gemini sessions where the model's reasoning
prose is stored separately. Supports both plain-string and structured-array
thought formats (subject/description objects).

The list command's assistant character count now includes tool_result content
in its tally, preventing artificially low avg-chars-per-response metrics for
sessions with heavy tool use.

A new factory_tool_uses_from_file() counter is registered for the "factory"
provider slug so --tool-stats work correctly.

All changes include comprehensive unit tests covering the new parsing paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
